sections.heading: RT
sections.content:
Principles: Individualize RT planning and delivery. Use CT-based treatment planning. Whole breast RT is typically delivered with hypofractionated schedules (preferred). A boost to the tumor bed is recommended in patients at higher risk for recurrence. Regional nodal irradiation (RNI) is recommended based on nodal status and tumor factors. Sequencing of RT with systemic therapy: RT typically follows chemotherapy if indicated. For patients with a history of RT to the breast, re-irradiation may be considered with careful assessment of prior dose and volume.

Dose Frameworks:
1. Whole Breast RT (Hypofractionated): Total dose 40–42.5 Gy, dose per fraction 2.6–2.83 Gy, fractions 15–16, schedule Daily over 3–3.2 weeks, indication Preferred for most patients after BCS.
2. Whole Breast RT (Ultra-Hypofractionated): Total dose 28.5 Gy or 26 Gy, dose per fraction 5.7 Gy or 5.2 Gy, fractions 5, schedule 28.5 Gy given once weekly; 26 Gy given daily, indication Select patients with pTis/T1/T2/N0 aged >50 years after BCS.
3. Tumor Bed Boost: Total dose 10–16 Gy, dose per fraction 2 Gy, fractions 5–8, schedule Sequential after whole breast RT, indication Recommended for higher-risk features.
4. APBI/PBI (External Beam): Total dose 30 Gy or 40 Gy, dose per fraction 6 Gy or 2.67 Gy, fractions 5 or 15, schedule 30 Gy: every other day; 40 Gy: daily, indication Appropriately selected low-risk patients meeting ASTRO criteria.

Approaches:
1. Post-BCS Radiation: Dose fractionation 40–42.5 Gy in 15–16 fractions (preferred). Indication All patients after BCS for invasive cancer or DCIS unless contraindicated. Key trial EBCTCG meta-analysis: reduces 10-year recurrence risk. Toxicities: acute skin erythema, fatigue, breast edema; late breast fibrosis, etc.
2. Post-Mastectomy Radiation Therapy (PMRT): Dose fractionation 45–50.4 Gy in 25–28 fractions or 40–42.5 Gy in 15–16 fractions to chest wall. Indication Patients with positive nodes (pN1mi+risk factors, pN1a, pN2-3), positive margins, or T3/T4. Key trial EBCTCG meta-analysis: reduces 20-year breast cancer mortality in 1-3 positive nodes. Toxicities: acute skin erythema, desquamation, fatigue; late lymphedema, etc.
3. Regional Nodal Irradiation (RNI): Dose fractionation 46–50 Gy in 23–25 fractions or 39–42.5 Gy in 15–16 fractions. Indication Node-positive disease (≥4 positive nodes strongly recommended; 1-3 may be considered). May be considered for node-negative with high-risk features. Key trials MA.20, EORTC 22922. Toxicities: increased risk of lymphedema, pneumonitis, brachial plexopathy, cardiac toxicity.

timeline.title: Post-BCS Radiation
timeline.body: Dose: 40–42.5 Gy in 15–16 fractions (preferred). Indication: All patients after BCS for invasive cancer or DCIS unless contraindicated.

timeline.title: Post-Mastectomy RT (PMRT)
timeline.body: Dose: 45–50.4 Gy in 25–28 fractions or 40–42.5 Gy in 15–16 fractions. Indication: Positive nodes (pN1a+, pN2-3), positive margins, T3/T4.

timeline.title: Regional Nodal Irradiation (RNI)
timeline.body: Dose: 46–50 Gy in 23–25 fractions or 39–42.5 Gy in 15–16 fractions. Indication: Node-positive disease (≥4 nodes strongly recommended; 1-3 may be considered).

timeline.title: Tumor Bed Boost
timeline.body: Dose: 10–16 Gy in 5–8 fractions (sequential after whole breast RT). Indication: Higher-risk features (age <50, high-grade, close/positive margins).

timeline.title: APBI/PBI (External Beam)
timeline.body: Dose: 30 Gy (5 fractions every other day) or 40 Gy (15 fractions daily). Indication: Low-risk patients meeting ASTRO criteria.
